seoTitle: Insert an Image in a Confluence Page (2026 Tutorial)
metaDescription: Learn how to insert an image in a Confluence page in 2026, from dragging files or pasting URLs to adjusting alignment, sizing, and alt text before publishing.
intro: Visual guides make documentation far easier to follow, but poorly placed images can wreck a page's layout. This tutorial covers how to insert, align, and size images inside a Confluence page using the 2026 editor tools.

steps.heading: Open the image uploader
steps.body: In edit mode, click your insertion point and type the `/media` or `/image` slash command to open the file uploader frame.

steps.heading: Add your image
steps.body: Drag and drop an image file from your desktop, or paste a secure web URL to pull it in directly.

steps.heading: Set alignment
steps.body: Use the floating bottom controls to align the image left, center, or right on the page.

steps.heading: Scale the image
steps.body: Drag the outer edge handles to adjust the width, or toggle the full-width option for larger visuals.

steps.heading: Add alt text
steps.body: Click the image to open its settings and add descriptive alt text for accessibility and search visibility.

steps.heading: Publish the page
steps.body: Click Publish to make the finished, responsive layout live.

faq.question: Can I paste an image directly from a web URL?
faq.answer: Yes, the media block accepts a pasted web URL in addition to drag-and-drop uploads from your desktop.

faq.question: How do I keep large images from slowing down my page?
faq.answer: Confluence's 2026 rendering engine compresses media so large graphics load quickly without lagging the surrounding text.

faq.question: Can I place images inside a table or multi-column layout?
faq.answer: Yes, native alignment panels let you place images inside multi-column tables and section blocks.

faq.question: Why should I add alt text to my images?
faq.answer: Alt text improves accessibility for screen readers and helps search engines understand the image content.
